regions.text: We may learn from photosynthesis how to grow more food. More than half the population is short of food. The need for new supplies will increase.
Much photosynthesis (90 per cent) goes on in microscopic plants in the sea. Future food may come from the oceans.
Making food from carbon, hydrogen, and oxygen may become a new industry. At present we burn coal and oil for energy. microscopic: too small to see without a microscope. maybe: possibly.
oil: liquids used for fuel. Gasoline is 'gas' in the U.S.A. and 'petrol' in Britain.
